config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi agar tidak berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan minoritas atau membungkam suara yang berbeda. Dalam praktiknya, saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan, dengan hak individu sebagai batas yang jelas bagi tindakan negara dan masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of statement sounds too close to giving the majority a blank check to override minorities, unpopular speech, or basic civil liberties whenever it claims a “greater good.” Society absolutely has legitimate interests in safety, public health, and order, but those interests should be pursued within firm limits that protect individual dignity and freedom. In practice, I would put individual rights first and only accept limits when they are narrowly necessary and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, apalagi terhadap kelompok kecil, kritik, atau kebebasan berpendapat. Memang ada situasi seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang butuh pembatasan, tetapi itu harus sangat terbatas, jelas dasar hukumnya, dan tidak boleh menghapus martabat orang per orang. Buat saya, masyarakat kuat justru ketika hak individu dilindungi, bukan ketika individu diminta terus mengalah tanpa batas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From a U.S. perspective, we have seen too many examples of “for the common good” being used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuses of power, which is exactly why constitutional rights are supposed to act as a hard limit on majorities and governments. That said, I do not treat individual rights as absolute in every case; public health, emergency safety, and basic order can justify narrow restrictions when they are truly necessary and temporary. What I reject is the idea that society gets automatic priority, because once that becomes the default, minority rights become fragile and easy to dismiss.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan hak orang per orang, terutama kelompok kecil atau suara yang kritis. Kebutuhan publik seperti kesehatan, keamanan, dan ketertiban memang nyata, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan harus ketat, sementara, dan punya dasar hukum yang jelas, bukan diberi prioritas otomatis di atas hak individu. Buat saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi martabat setiap warganya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American point of view, history makes me wary of any broad claim that the greater good should override rights, because that logic has been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. I do think there are narrow exceptions for public health, emergency safety, and order, but those limits have to be temporary, specific, and carefully justified. If society always comes first, then individual rights stop being real protections and become privileges the majority can take away whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
